maxContractTerm.value: Five (5) years default maximum; up to seven (7) years if approved by the appropriate chief procurement officer (under the South Carolina Consolidated Procurement Code) or by the Superintendent (under a district-adopted procurement code that mirrors the Model); longer than seven years only if approved by the board (State Fiscal Accountability Authority for in-Code districts; the school board for districts operating under a district code mirroring the 2021 Model SDPC). Title 59, Chapter 67 (the school-transportation statute, including Section 59-67-460 authorizing private-contractor transportation contracts) is silent on contract term; the cap flows from the Consolidated Procurement Code (S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-2030(4)-(5)) and applies to (a) school districts whose annual expenditures exceed $75 million (subject to the Code per Section 11-35-5340) unless they have adopted a Division-of-Procurement-Services-approved substantially-similar own code, and (b) districts that have adopted a Model-Code-derived district code (Reg. 19-445.3000; 2021 Model SDPC Section 2030(4)-(5), which sets the same 5-year default / 7-year Superintendent-approved cap and requires school-board approval beyond seven years). Context note: SC school bus transportation is predominantly delivered through a state-owned/leased bus fleet under SBE/SCDE authority (Sections 59-67-410, 59-67-420; SBE Reg. 43-80), so private district-level contracting under Section 59-67-460 historically occurs as supplemental, special-needs, or alternative service.
maxContractTerm.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-2030(4) ("The maximum potential duration for a contract is five years. A maximum potential duration of up to seven years may be approved by the appropriate chief procurement officer.") and Section 11-35-2030(5) (board approval required beyond seven years); applicability to school districts via Section 11-35-5340 ($75M expenditure threshold subjects district to Chapter 35 unless district has substantially-similar own code), Section 11-35-310(24) (school districts are "political subdivisions") and Section 11-35-310(18) (Governmental body excludes school districts), and Section 11-35-5320 (all political subdivisions to adopt procurement ordinances embodying "sound principles of appropriately competitive procurement"); S.C. Code Regs. 19-445.2135(G) ("Every contract with a total potential duration in excess of five years must be approved as required by Section 11-35-2030(4) or Section 11-35-2030(5)"); 2021 Model School District Procurement Code Section 2030(4)-(5) (parallels Section 11-35-2030 with Superintendent substituted for chief procurement officer).
maxContractTerm.sourceTier: Tier 1 (codified statute) + Tier 2 (administrative regulation 19-445.2135 and 19-445.3000) + Tier 4 (SCSFAA Division of Procurement Services 2021 Model School District Procurement Code)

rebidRequired.value: Conditional. South Carolina's school-transportation statute (Title 59, Chapter 67, including Section 59-67-460 authorizing private-contractor transportation contracts) is silent on rebidding at term end. Rebid obligations flow from the Consolidated Procurement Code (or, for in-Code districts that have adopted their own substantially-similar code, from that district code). Under Section 11-35-1510, all state contracts must be awarded by competitive sealed bidding except as provided in sixteen enumerated alternative source-selection methods, and Section 11-35-1520(1) provides the default rule. Because Section 11-35-2030(4)-(5) caps a single contract's maximum potential duration (5 years default; 7 years with CPO approval; longer only with board approval), at the end of the awarded maximum potential duration a new competitive source-selection action is required unless an enumerated exception applies (e.g., competitive sealed proposals under Section 11-35-1530, competitive negotiations under Section 11-35-1535, negotiations after unsuccessful sealed bidding under Section 11-35-1540, small purchases under Section 11-35-1550, sole source under Section 11-35-1560, emergency under Section 11-35-1570, or cooperative purchasing under Article 19 of Chapter 35). For school districts below the $75M threshold that have not adopted a Code-substantially-similar ordinance, Section 11-35-5320 requires adoption of ordinances or procedures embodying "sound principles of appropriately competitive procurement," but the same section provides that "[a] political subdivision's failure to adopt appropriate ordinances, procedures, or policies of procurement is not subject to the legal remedies provided in this code."
rebidRequired.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-1510 (methods of source selection; sixteen enumerated alternatives to competitive sealed bidding); Section 11-35-1520(1) (competitive sealed bidding default); Section 11-35-2030 (multiterm contracts; 5/7-year max potential duration); Section 11-35-5320 (political subdivisions required to develop and adopt procurement laws; failure not subject to legal remedies); Section 11-35-5340 ($75M threshold; school district subject to Code unless substantially-similar own code); Section 59-67-460 (county board of education authority to contract with private contractors for transportation services).

renewalMechanics.value: Renewal without a new competitive solicitation is permitted only within a contract's pre-established maximum potential duration. Under S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-2030(1), "[t]he term of the contract and conditions of renewal or extension must be included in the solicitation and funds must be available for the first fiscal period at the time of contracting. Payment and performance obligations for succeeding fiscal periods must be subject to the availability and appropriation of funds for them." Before using a multiterm contract the governmental body must determine in writing (a) that estimated requirements cover the period and are reasonably firm and continuing and (b) that the contract serves the State's interest by encouraging effective competition or otherwise promoting economies (Section 11-35-2030(2)). If funds are not appropriated for a succeeding fiscal period, the contract must be cancelled (Section 11-35-2030(3); Reg. 19-445.2135(E)(3)). Total maximum potential duration (initial term plus all renewal/extension options) is capped at five years by default, seven years with chief procurement officer (or, under the Model SDPC, Superintendent) approval, and beyond seven years only with board (SCFAA for in-Code districts; the school board under a Model-Code-derived district code) approval (Section 11-35-2030(4)-(5); Reg. 19-445.2135(G); 2021 Model SDPC Section 2030(4)-(5)). The Code does not by statute prescribe CPI/fuel escalator mechanics, automatic evergreen rollover, or annual one-year extensions; those are matters of solicitation drafting (allowed if disclosed in the solicitation per Section 11-35-2030(1)). Title 59, Chapter 67 imposes no separate renewal mechanic; Section 59-67-460 says only that the county board of education "shall execute the contracts" with private transportation contractors.
renewalMechanics.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-2030(1)-(5); S.C. Code Regs. 19-445.2135 (Conditions for Use of Multi-Term Contracts; including Sec. 19-445.2135(G) ("Every contract with a total potential duration in excess of five years must be approved as required by Section 11-35-2030(4) or Section 11-35-2030(5)")); 2021 Model School District Procurement Code Section 2030(1)-(5).
renewalMechanics.sourceTier: Tier 1 (codified statute) + Tier 2 (administrative regulation 19-445.2135) + Tier 4 (2021 Model SDPC)

statutoryCitation.value: Primary transportation-authority statute: S.C. Code Ann. Section 59-67-460 (Transportation by private contractor) ("Any county board of education may at any time contract for any part or all of its transportation services with private individuals or contractors for the furnishing of such services. In any such instance the county board of education shall execute the contracts.") -- silent on term, bidding, and renewal. Primary procurement statutes establishing term, rebid, and renewal mechanics (Title 59 Chapter 67 being silent, and the procurement code defining "political subdivision" to include school districts at Section 11-35-310(24), with Section 11-35-5340 expressly applying the Code to school districts above the $75M expenditure threshold absent an approved substantially-similar local code): S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-2030 (Multiterm contracts; 5-year default cap; 7-year CPO-approved; longer-than-7-year board-approved); Section 11-35-1510 (Methods of source selection); Section 11-35-1520 (Competitive sealed bidding); Section 11-35-5320 (Political subdivisions required to adopt procurement laws); Section 11-35-5340 (School district subject to Consolidated Procurement Code; $75M threshold and substantially-similar exemption mechanism); Section 11-35-310(18) ("Governmental body" excludes school districts) and Section 11-35-310(24) ("Political subdivision" includes school districts). Implementing regulations: S.C. Code Regs. 19-445.2135 (Multi-Term Contracts) and 19-445.3000 (School District Procurement Codes; Model). State-published model: 2021 Model School District Procurement Code (SCSFAA Division of Procurement Services) Section 2030(4) ("Maximum Duration. The maximum potential duration for a contract is five years. A maximum potential duration of up to seven years may be approved by the Superintendent.") and Section 2030(5) (board approval beyond seven years).
statutoryCitation.sourceTier: Tier 1 (codified statutes) + Tier 2 (Title 19 Chapter 445, S.C. Code of Regulations) + Tier 4 (SCSFAA Division of Procurement Services 2021 Model School District Procurement Code)

districtVariance.value: Yes. South Carolina's statutory framework expressly contemplates variance among school districts. Under S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-310(18), "Governmental body" excludes "all local political subdivisions such as counties, municipalities, school districts." School districts are subject to the Consolidated Procurement Code only via Section 11-35-5340 -- i.e., when annual expenditures (including debt service) exceed $75 million. A district above that threshold may further exempt itself from the Code (other than a triennial procurement audit) by adopting its own procurement code that the SCSFAA Division of Procurement Services finds, in writing, to be "substantially similar." Districts below the $75M threshold are required by Section 11-35-5320 to adopt procurement ordinances or procedures embodying "sound principles of appropriately competitive procurement," but the same section provides that "[a] political subdivision's failure to adopt appropriate ordinances, procedures, or policies of procurement is not subject to the legal remedies provided in this code." Reg. 19-445.3000(C) explicitly acknowledges that a district code "may accommodate the differing context of school districts (e.g., differences between state government and local school district operations, including size, purchasing staff resources, volume and type of procurements, and structure of its governing body and executive hierarchy)." Two confirming examples: Greenville County School District's procurement code (adopted January 21, 2026) and School District Five of Lexington and Richland Counties (approved March 3, 2008) each mirror the 5/7-year cap and the Model SDPC's substitution of "Superintendent" for "chief procurement officer" in Section 2030(4). The SCSFAA's publication of the 2021 Model SDPC (Reg. 19-445.3000(E)) is intended to drive uniformity, but adoption outside the $75M threshold is voluntary.
districtVariance.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Sections 11-35-310(18), 11-35-310(24), 11-35-5320, 11-35-5340; S.C. Code Regs. 19-445.3000(A), (C), (E); SCSFAA Division of Procurement Services, 2021 Model School District Procurement Code; Greenville County School District Procurement Code (eff. January 21, 2026); School District Five of Lexington and Richland Counties Procurement Code (March 3, 2008 written opinion).
districtVariance.sourceTier: Tier 1 (codified statute) + Tier 2 (administrative regulation 19-445.3000) + Tier 4 (Model SDPC and verified adopting districts' codes)

specialEducation.explanation: South Carolina law does not set different contract-term, rebid, renewal, or procurement rules for special-education / IEP / McKinney-Vento / foster-care / disability student transportation contracts between districts and private contractors. The duty to transport students with disabilities is established at S.C. Code Ann. Section 59-67-520 ("The State Department of Education shall have the responsibility for transporting handicapped persons of lawful school age to and from the nearest school in which a handicapped pupil has been duly assigned by the proper authorities, but the State Board of Education by appropriate regulation may make exceptions in particular cases."), and federal IDEA imposes a duty to provide transportation as a related service when required by an IEP. Both go to the DUTY to transport, not to the contract framework. State Board Regulation 43-80 (Operation of Public Student Transportation Services), Section XXV.B addresses contract transportation only for individual hard-to-reach students transported by parent or individual contract (per-mile reimbursement, 90-school-day periodicity), not large-scale private-bus-contractor procurement. Procurement of transportation contracts that may carry special-education students follows the same framework: Sections 11-35-1510, 11-35-1520, 11-35-2030 (5/7-year cap, multiterm rules) where the Consolidated Procurement Code applies via Section 11-35-5340, or under a district's own substantially-similar ordinance via Section 11-35-5320. No special-education-specific carve-out from Section 11-35-2030 was located. Federal IDEA (34 C.F.R. Part 300) and McKinney-Vento (42 U.S.C. Section 11431 et seq.) impose duty-to-transport rules but do not displace state contract-term or procurement rules.
specialEducation.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 59-67-520; S.C. State Board of Education Reg. 43-80 Section XXV.B; S.C. Code Ann. Sections 11-35-1510, 11-35-1520, 11-35-2030, 11-35-5320, 11-35-5340.

maxContractTerm.note: Section 11-35-2030(4)-(5) text re-opened at scstatehouse.gov/code/t11c035.php and matched verbatim ('The maximum potential duration for a contract is five years. A maximum potential duration of up to seven years may be approved by the appropriate chief procurement officer.'). Reg. 19-445.2135(G) text re-opened in the official Chapter 19 PDF and matches. 2021 Model SDPC Section 2030(4)-(5) re-opened on procurement.sc.gov and matches verbatim. Title 59 Ch. 67 confirmed silent on contract term via direct review.

rebidRequired.note: Sections 11-35-1510 (16 enumerated alternatives) and 11-35-1520(1) (competitive sealed bidding default) re-opened and confirmed verbatim. Section 11-35-2030 5/7-year cap forces rebid at end of maximum potential duration unless an enumerated source-selection alternative applies. Section 11-35-5320's 'failure not subject to legal remedies' rider re-opened and quoted. No statutory school-bus carve-out from competitive bidding located.

renewalMechanics.note: Section 11-35-2030(1)-(5) full text re-verified verbatim. Reg. 19-445.2135 paragraphs (D)-(G) re-opened in the official Chapter 19 PDF; (G) text matches Stage 1 framing. Annual-appropriations cancellation rule re-verified at Section 11-35-2030(3) and Reg. 19-445.2135(E)(3). No statutory CPI/fuel/evergreen mandate located; consistent with Stage 1.

statutoryCitation.note: All seven cited statute sections (59-67-460; 11-35-310(18) and (24); 11-35-1510; 11-35-1520; 11-35-2030; 11-35-5320; 11-35-5340) re-opened and quoted verbatim. All two cited regulations (19-445.2135; 19-445.3000) re-opened in the official Chapter 19 PDF. 2021 Model SDPC verified as published by SCSFAA Division of Procurement Services.

districtVariance.note: Statutory framework verified via Sections 11-35-310(18)/(24), 11-35-5320, 11-35-5340. Reg. 19-445.3000(C) verbatim language about accommodating 'differing context of school districts' confirmed in the official PDF. Two concrete adopting districts located and re-opened: Greenville County School District (eff. Jan 21, 2026) and School District Five of Lexington and Richland Counties (March 3, 2008 written opinion). Both mirror the 5/7-year cap and the Model's substitution of Superintendent for CPO -- consistent with Stage 1's variance characterization.

specialEducation.note: Section 59-67-520 text re-verified at scstatehouse.gov. Reg. 43-80 Section XXV (Contract Transportation) text re-opened and confirmed to address parent/individual-contractor transportation of single hard-to-reach students, not large-scale private-bus-contractor procurement. No separate IDEA/IEP/McKinney-Vento/foster carve-out from Section 11-35-2030 located in either the statute or the State Board regulation.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term -- 5-year default / 7-year CPO-approved / >7-year board-approved cap
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Title 59, Chapter 67 (school transportation) may contain a specific carve-out establishing a longer maximum term for school-bus contracts, or Section 11-35-2030 may have been amended after 2019 Act 41 to alter the 5/7-year limits.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened scstatehouse.gov Title 59 Chapter 67 in full; grepped for 'term', 'years', 'not exceed', 'multi-year', 'extension', 'transportation contract'. Re-opened scstatehouse.gov Title 11 Chapter 35; grepped 11-35-2030 history and Effect of Amendment notes. Searched H.3242 (2025-2026) for any new contract-term language.
challengeMatrix.result: No counter-evidence. Title 59 Chapter 67 contains no contract-term provision. 11-35-2030 most recent amendment is 2019 Act 41 Section 33, eff May 13, 2019; (4) was rewritten and (5) added by that Act to create the current 5/7/board ladder. H.3242 is unenacted and does not specify contract-term caps.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid Required -- Conditional (rebid at end of maximum potential duration unless enumerated exception applies)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Section 11-35-1510 may contain a school-bus or transportation-specific carve-out from competitive sealed bidding; cooperative purchasing under Article 19 or a piggyback authority may broadly displace rebid obligations; sole-source authority under Section 11-35-1560 may be commonly used for transportation.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened Section 11-35-1510 (sixteen enumerated alternatives) and Section 11-35-1520. Re-opened Section 11-35-4810 (cooperative purchasing). Confirmed no transportation-specific exception. Reviewed Section 11-35-1560 for any narrowing.
challengeMatrix.result: No counter-evidence. 16 enumerated alternatives are general-purpose; cooperative purchasing/sole-source/emergency exist but are conditions, not blanket exceptions. Stage 1's 'Conditional' framing accurately reflects the statutory structure.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics -- renewal/extension permitted only within the pre-disclosed maximum potential duration; no statutory CPI/fuel/evergreen mandate
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Statute or regulation may impose CPI caps, fuel-escalator rules, public-hearing requirements before renewal, max number of renewals, automatic appropriation triggers, or annual termination notice requirements.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened 11-35-2030(1)-(5) and Reg. 19-445.2135(A)-(G). Searched for 'CPI', 'fuel', 'escalator', 'public hearing', 'evergreen', 'rollover', 'annual termination', 'one-year'. Searched Title 59 Chapter 67 for any school-transportation renewal mechanic.
challengeMatrix.result: No counter-evidence. 11-35-2030(1) leaves renewal/extension terms to solicitation drafting; (3) ties multi-term contracts to annual appropriations (cancellation if funds unavailable); (4)-(5) cap total potential duration. No statutory CPI/fuel/evergreen rule located.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Statutory Citation -- 59-67-460 plus the 11-35 series and the 19-445 regulations are the controlling authorities
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Another statute (e.g., a Title 59 provision other than 59-67-460 or a Title 11 provision other than the cited ones) may control; the cited sections may have been amended or repealed after the code-currency date.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened each cited section verbatim. Reviewed 'HISTORY' and 'Effect of Amendment' notes for each on scstatehouse.gov. Searched legislative bill database for amendments to each cited section since 2019 Act 41 effective date (May 13, 2019).
challengeMatrix.result: No counter-evidence. All seven statute sections verified verbatim. Most recent comprehensive amendment is 2019 Act 41, eff May 13, 2019. No later session law amends the cited substantive language.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-Education Differentiation -- No separate contract framework for special-education student transportation
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: S.C. State Board Reg. 43-80, the SC Department of Education's special-education manual, or federal IDEA/McKinney-Vento may impose distinct contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules for special-education / IEP / McKinney-Vento / foster-care / homeless / alternative transportation.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened Reg. 43-80 (Operation of Public Student Transportation Services); grepped for 'IEP', 'special education', 'disability', 'McKinney', 'foster', 'homeless', 'alternative', 'contract term', 'rebid', 'renewal'. Re-opened Section 59-67-520. Considered federal IDEA / 34 C.F.R. Part 300 transportation-as-related-service rules.
challengeMatrix.result: No counter-evidence. Reg. 43-80 Section XXV.B addresses parent/individual-contractor transportation for single hard-to-reach students (per-mile reimbursement; 90-school-day cap), not private-bus-contractor procurement. Section 59-67-520 addresses State Department duty to transport, not contract framework. IDEA/McKinney-Vento create duties; they do not preempt state contract/procurement rules.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: District Variance -- Yes; statutory framework expressly authorizes district variance
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: All SC school districts in practice apply the same 5/7-year cap and competitive-bidding default uniformly (so 'variance' is only theoretical), or only one of the two-tier ($75M) classification has practical effect.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened Greenville County School District Procurement Code (eff. Jan 21, 2026) -- Section 2030 mirrors Model SDPC 5/7-year structure with Superintendent substitution. Re-opened School District Five of Lexington and Richland Counties Procurement Code (March 3, 2008 written opinion) -- Section 2030.4 'Maximum Time For Use' identical structure. Considered whether districts below $75M and without adopted codes might apply different practices.
challengeMatrix.result: Both examined district codes mirror the Model 5/7-year structure (supporting in-Code uniformity). However, statutory framework still expressly authorizes (i) sub-$75M districts to adopt non-mirroring procedures (and Section 11-35-5320 explicitly bars legal remedies for non-adoption), and (ii) Reg. 19-445.3000(C) permits the district code to 'accommodate the differing context of school districts.' Variance authorization remains in the statute even where examined districts have aligned.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Original finding confirmed; no impact. Note added that examined large-district codes mirror the Model 5/7 cap.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A post-2019 session law (2020-2026) may have amended 11-35-2030, 11-35-5320, 11-35-5340, 59-67-460, or 59-67-520, or H.3242 (2025-2026) may have been enacted, displacing the cited framework.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Reviewed scstatehouse.gov bill search for any enacted amendment to Title 11 Chapter 35 and Title 59 Chapter 67 from 2019 through June 2026. Re-opened H.3242 (2025-2026) status page; confirmed bill is 'Introduced and read first time' Jan 14, 2025, currently in House Ways and Means; NOT ENACTED. Checked for any enacted school-bus privatization or transportation-procurement bill since 2019.
challengeMatrix.result: No counter-evidence. No enacted session law since 2019 Act 41 amends the cited substantive provisions. H.3242 remains in committee; predecessor bills (H.4582 2023-2024, H.4606 2023-2024, H.4389 2017-2018, H.4610 2011-2012, H.3220 2019-2020) expired without enactment. Reg. Chapter 19 last amended SCSR 44-6 Doc. No. 4894, eff June 26, 2020.

sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Last amended 2019 Act No. 41 (S.530), Section 33, eff May 13, 2019; applies to solicitations issued after that date (2019 Act No. 41, Section 80).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Renewal Mechanics; Rebid Required at Term-End
sourceLedger.relevantText: (4) Maximum Duration. The maximum potential duration for a contract is five years. A maximum potential duration of up to seven years may be approved by the appropriate chief procurement officer. (5) Authority Approval. Every type of contract with a maximum potential duration exceeding seven years must be approved by the board. For competitive procurements, approval of the maximum potential duration must be granted before solicitation.

sourceLedger.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-5340 (School district subject to consolidated procurement code; exemptions)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Formerly Code 1976 Section 11-35-70, amended and recodified by 2019 Act No. 41 (S.530), Sections 4 and 79, eff May 13, 2019.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Applicability of procurement code to school districts; District Variance
sourceLedger.relevantText: Irrespective of the source of funds, any school district whose budget of total expenditures, including debt service, exceeds seventy-five million dollars annually is subject to the provisions of Chapter 35, Title 11... However, if a district has its own procurement code which is... substantially similar to the provisions of the South Carolina Consolidated Procurement Code, the district is exempt... except for a procurement audit which must be performed every three years.

sourceLedger.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-5320 (Political subdivisions required to develop and adopt procurement laws)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Formerly Code 1976 Section 11-35-50, recodified by 2019 Act No. 41 (S.530), Section 79, eff May 13, 2019.
sourceLedger.relevantText: All political subdivisions of the State shall adopt ordinances or procedures embodying sound principles of appropriately competitive procurement no later than July 1, 1983... A political subdivision's failure to adopt appropriate ordinances, procedures, or policies of procurement is not subject to the legal remedies provided in this code.

sourceLedger.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-310(18) and (24) (Definitions: 'Governmental body' / 'Political subdivision')
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Amended 2019 Act No. 41 (S.530), Section 6, eff May 13, 2019.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Applicability of procurement code to school districts
sourceLedger.relevantText: (18) 'Governmental body' means... Governmental body excludes... all local political subdivisions such as counties, municipalities, school districts, or public service or special purpose districts... (24) 'Political subdivision' means all counties, municipalities, school districts, public service or special purpose districts.

sourceLedger.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 11-35-1510 (Methods of source selection) and Section 11-35-1520 (Competitive sealed bidding)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Most recently amended 2019 Act No. 41 (S.530), Section 17, eff May 13, 2019.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Section 11-35-1510: Unless otherwise provided by law, all state contracts must be awarded by competitive sealed bidding, pursuant to Section 11-35-1520, except as provided in [16 enumerated alternatives]... Section 11-35-1520(1) Condition for Use. Contracts must be awarded by competitive sealed bidding except as otherwise provided in Section 11-35-1510.

sourceLedger.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 59-67-460 (Contracts with private individuals or contractors for school transportation)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Title 59 Chapter 67 last shown amendment 2009 Act No. 17 (Section 59-67-535); Section 59-67-460 unchanged since enactment.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory authority for district-private contractor transportation arrangements; Statute silent on contract term, rebid, renewal
sourceLedger.relevantText: Any county board of education may at any time contract for any part or all of its transportation services with private individuals or contractors for the furnishing of such services. In any such instance the county board of education shall execute the contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: S.C. Code Ann. Section 59-67-520 (Transportation of handicapped students)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Statutory text traces to 1962 Code Section 21-833; (39) 1965 (54) 75; (44) 1968 (55) 2697.
sourceLedger.relevantText: The State Department of Education shall have the responsibility for transporting handicapped persons of lawful school age to and from the nearest school in which a handicapped pupil has been duly assigned by the proper authorities...

sourceLedger.citation: S.C. Code Regs. 19-445.2135 (Conditions for Use of Multi-Term Contracts)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Last amended SCSR 44-6 Doc. No. 4894, eff June 26, 2020.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Renewal Mechanics; Max Contract Term; conditions for multi-term contracts
sourceLedger.relevantText: A multi-term contract is a contract for the acquisition of supplies, services, or information technology for more than one year... (E)(3) that the multi-term contract will be cancelled only if funds are not appropriated... (G) Maximum Contract Periods. Every contract with a total potential duration in excess of five years must be approved as required by Section 11-35-2030(4) or Section 11-35-2030(5).

sourceLedger.citation: S.C. Code Regs. 19-445.3000 (School District Procurement Codes; Model)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Added by State Register Vol. 31, Issue No. 5, eff May 25, 2007; amended SCSR 44-4 Doc. No. 4861, eff April 24, 2020; SCSR 44-6 Doc. No. 4894, eff June 26, 2020.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: District Variance; applicability of Code to school districts; substantially-similar standard
sourceLedger.relevantText: (A) Under Section 11-35-5340, a school district is exempt from the South Carolina Consolidated Procurement Code (except for a procurement audit) if the district has its own procurement code which is... substantially similar to the provisions of the Consolidated Procurement Code and regulations... (C) To qualify for approval, a district code should largely mirror, but need not be identical to, the Consolidated Procurement Code... a district code may accommodate the differing context of school districts...

sourceLedger.citation: S.C. State Board of Education Reg. 43-80 (Operation of Public Student Transportation Services), Section XXV (Contract Transportation)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: South Carolina State Board of Education regulation; document metadata indicates 2021 modification.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special education / individual-contract transportation; not large private-bus-contractor procurement
sourceLedger.relevantText: XXV.B. Contract Transportation - Contract transportation will be limited to students who cannot be transported efficiently by state-owned/leased buses... [contracts for individual students with parents or other individuals, billed per mile, capped per 90 school days]

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 (official agency guidance citing binding authority)
sourceLedger.citation: South Carolina Division of Procurement Services (State Fiscal Accountability Authority), 2021 Model School District Procurement Code, Section 2030
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Published 2021; cites underlying S.C. Code provisions including Section 11-35-2030 as the source of its rule.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Renewal Mechanics; Rebid; District Variance
sourceLedger.relevantText: SECTION 2030. Multiterm contracts. (S.C. Code Section 11-35-2030)... (4) Maximum Duration. The maximum potential duration for a contract is five years. A maximum potential duration of up to seven years may be approved by the Superintendent. (5) Approval. Every type of contract with a maximum potential duration exceeding seven years must be approved by the board.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 (adopted district code; example of in-Code adoption)
sourceLedger.citation: Greenville County School District Procurement Code (eff. Jan 21, 2026), Section 2030
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: District Variance (by example of district adopting Model-aligned code)
sourceLedger.relevantText: SECTION 2030. Multiterm contracts. (S.C. Code Section 11-35-2030)... (4) Maximum Duration. The maximum potential duration for a contract is five years. A maximum potential duration of up to seven years may be approved by the Superintendent. (5) Approval. Every type of contract with a maximum potential duration exceeding seven years must be approved by the board.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 (adopted district code; example of district-code adoption)
sourceLedger.citation: School District Five of Lexington and Richland Counties Procurement Code (written opinion of approval issued March 3, 2008), Section 2030.4
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: District code approved March 3, 2008 (Materials Management Officer written opinion).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: District Variance (by example of district adopting Model-aligned code)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 2030.4 Maximum Time For Use. The maximum time for a multi-term contract is five (5) years. Contract terms of up to seven (7) years may be approved by the Superintendent. Contracts exceeding seven (7) years may be approved by the Board. (SC Code Section 11-35-2030(4); SC Reg. Section 19-445.2135(D))

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Pending legislation (NOT current law; recorded for context only)
sourceLedger.citation: S.C. H.3242 (126th General Assembly, 2025-2026) -- 'South Carolina School Bus Privatization Act of 2025'
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Prefiled Dec 5, 2024; introduced and read first time Jan 14, 2025; referred to House Committee on Ways and Means; NOT ENACTED as of 2026-06-24.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Not current law. Recorded to document that, as of 2026-06-24, no enacted bill displaces the framework above.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Would require school districts (beginning 2026-2027, no later than 2028-2029) to prepare and publish RFPs for school transportation services if they elect not to provide such services themselves. Bill does not specify a contract-term cap.

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Stage 2 re-opened every Stage 1 citation at the official source and confirmed every quoted excerpt matched verbatim. Two principal documents (S.C. Statehouse Title 11 Chapter 35 page and S.C. Code Regs Chapter 19 PDF) were each fetched once and grep-searched for every relevant section/subsection to avoid redundant fetches (per execution-efficiency note 3). The 2021 Model SDPC PDF and two adopted-district PDFs (Greenville County School District, School District Five of Lexington and Richland Counties) were parsed to corroborate the Model framework and to spot-test district variance.
queriesThatMattered: Direct grep of the official Title 11 Chapter 35 page for SECTION 11-35-2030, 11-35-5340, 11-35-5320, 11-35-310, 11-35-1510, 11-35-1520
Direct grep of the official Chapter 19 regulations PDF for 19-445.2135 and 19-445.3000
Direct grep of the 2021 Model SDPC PDF for SECTION 2030 verbatim
WebFetch of Title 59 Chapter 67 to confirm Section 59-67-460 (private-contractor authority) and Section 59-67-520 (handicapped-student duty)
Status check of S.C. H.3242 (2025-2026) on the official legislature site
verificationNotes.codeCurrency: S.C. Code of Laws Title 11, Chapter 35 last comprehensively amended by 2019 Act No. 41 (S.530), eff May 13, 2019; applies to solicitations issued after that date (Section 80). Title 59, Chapter 67 last shown amendment is 2009 Act No. 17 (Section 59-67-535); Section 59-67-460 is unchanged since original enactment. The official S.C. Statehouse page presents these statutes as 'Unannotated' but as the legislature's currently published code.
verificationNotes.adminCodeCurrency: S.C. Code Regs. Chapter 19, Articles 5 and 7 (19-445 series, Consolidated Procurement Code regulations), last amended SCSR 44-6 Doc. No. 4894, eff June 26, 2020 (and for 19-445.3000, also amended SCSR 44-4 Doc. No. 4861, eff April 24, 2020). SBE Reg. 43-80 last modification per SCDOE document metadata indicates 2021.
verificationNotes.sessionLawCheck: Searched scstatehouse.gov bill search for amendments to Title 11 Chapter 35 and Title 59 Chapter 67 from May 13, 2019 forward through June 24, 2026. No enacted session law since 2019 Act 41 has amended Section 11-35-2030, Section 11-35-5340, Section 11-35-5320, Section 11-35-1510, Section 11-35-1520, Section 11-35-310, Section 59-67-460, or Section 59-67-520 in respects relevant to contract term, rebid, or renewal. H.3242 (School Bus Privatization Act of 2025) remains in House Ways and Means; predecessor bills H.4582 / H.4606 / H.4389 / H.4610 / H.3220 expired without enactment.
negativeSearchPaths: Searched scstatehouse.gov Title 59 Chapter 67 in full for any provision setting school-bus contract term limits, rebid requirements, renewal mechanics, or special-education-specific contracting carve-outs - none found beyond Section 59-67-460 (authority to contract) and Section 59-67-520 (handicapped transportation duty).

Searched SCDOE Regulation 43-80 in full for contract term/rebid/renewal/RFP rules governing district-private bus contractor procurement - found only Section XXV.B individual-student parent/contract transportation rules, not procurement rules.

Searched scstatehouse.gov bill database for any enacted school-bus privatization or transportation-procurement bill - H.3242 (2025-2026), H.4582 (2023-2024), H.4606 (2023-2024), H.4389 (2017-2018), H.4610 (2011-2012), H.3220 (2019-2020) all pending or expired without enactment.
Searched for SC AG opinions interpreting Section 11-35-2030 or Section 59-67-460 in the school-bus contract context - no AG opinion directly on point was located in open web search.

Searched for SCSBA (South Carolina School Boards Association) and SCASA (South Carolina Association of School Administrators) transportation-contract procurement guidance citing specific statutes - no targeted guidance located in open web search.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: Re-verified that Title 59 Chapter 67's silence on contract term/rebid/renewal does not conflict with Title 11 Chapter 35's framework -- the two are complementary: Chapter 67 authorizes the contractual relationship (Section 59-67-460) while Chapter 35 governs how the procurement is conducted when the Consolidated Procurement Code or a substantially-similar district code applies. No conflict between Section 11-35-2030(4)-(5) and Reg. 19-445.2135(G) -- the regulation reinforces the statute. No conflict between SBE Reg. 43-80 Section XXV.B and Chapter 35 -- Reg. 43-80 addresses a narrow per-mile parent/individual reimbursement mechanism, not procurement of private-bus-contractor services. No conflict with the 2021 Model SDPC -- the Model is published under Reg. 19-445.3000(E) and adapts the Consolidated Procurement Code rules for school districts by substituting 'Superintendent' for 'chief procurement officer' in Section 2030(4) and 'board' (i.e., the school board) for the SCSFAA board in Section 2030(5), all of which is consistent with the underlying statutory framework.
